items.label: Very large flow (132 actions)
items.desc: Total action count 132 exceeds the 50-action guideline. Split into child flows to improve testability and authoring performance.

items.label: Nesting depth 5
items.desc: Just over the 4-level guideline — review whether scopes can be flattened.

items.label: 12 sibling Scopes at root
items.desc: Many sibling scopes suggest the flow could be decomposed into child flows.

items.label: Hardcoded URLs in 3 action(s)
items.desc: Move endpoints to environment variables so they can be retargeted across DEV/TEST/PROD without editing the flow. Examples: HTTP_SendQuotation -> https://dml-dev.contoso.com/api/v1; HTTP_LookupCustomer -> https://crm-dev.contoso.com

items.label: Hardcoded email addresses (5 unique)
items.desc: Recipient addresses are baked into action inputs. Use environment variables or a Dataverse config table. Examples: ops-team@contoso.com, alerts@contoso.com

items.label: Magic numbers in expressions (4 unique)
items.desc: Large numeric literals appear inline (e.g. Set_variable_MaxRetries in 9303, Set_variable_TimeoutMs in 45000). Move policy thresholds to environment variables.

items.label: Manual trigger 'manual'
items.desc: Request triggers must enforce Entra ID / SAS / IP allow-list at the platform level. Verify auth posture outside the definition.

items.label: HTTP actions with authentication but no secureInputs (1)
items.desc: runtimeConfiguration.secureData.properties does not cover 'inputs'. Authentication blocks will be visible in run history. First: HTTP_SendQuotation

items.label: HTTP actions without explicit retryPolicy (1)
items.desc: The platform default applies, but explicit retry policies make behaviour deterministic. First: HTTP_SendQuotation

items.label: Foreach without explicit concurrency (4)
items.desc: Set runtimeConfiguration.concurrency explicitly to control parallelism. First: Apply_to_each_Order

items.label: Foreach loops with explicit concurrency (1)
items.desc: Good — concurrency is set deliberately. Example: Apply_to_each_LineItem
